api_policy: No model-generation API calls are authorized by this manifest. Paid runs require local checks, fresh pricing, balance check, and explicit user approval.

local_checks.name: lake build LeanNumerics.LeanNumBenchMinimalPairsV4
local_checks.notes: Initial build and post-ASCII-fix incremental build both completed; final explicit rerun completed successfully.

local_checks.name: no-LLM tactic baseline
local_checks.notes: Fixed tactic portfolio solved 0/16 targets: 0/8 easy and 0/8 hard, with --timeout 20.

local_checks.name: guided_v3 prompt preparation and audit
local_checks.notes: Generated 16 prompts; audit issues_count = 0; prompt chars min/mean/max = 4458/6893.9/12611.

local_checks.name: empty-template negative check
local_checks.notes: Normal checker passed 0/16 empty candidate proofs.

local_checks.name: statement elaboration positive check
local_checks.notes: Empty proofs treated as sorry passed 16/16 targets. V4P01_easy required the longer --timeout 240 check.

local_checks.name: prompt-field leak scan
local_checks.notes: No model-visible prompt/template hits for local paths, user or repository owner strings, API-key patterns, ground-truth v4 module name, or target theorem names.

local_checks.name: OpenRouter runner dry-run
local_checks.notes: Selected 16 GPT-5.5 standard prompts with max_paid_prompts=16 and made no API calls.

pairs.easy_surface: The outer heat step is exposed while the two-step grid values remain visible at adjacent indices.
pairs.hard_surface: The same three-step local heat stability fact is hidden behind nested heatStepGrid APIs.
pairs.equivalence_note: Both sides prove the same three-step heat-grid Linf bound from the same seven local input bounds.
pairs.risk_note: High-value stress pair; may still saturate if the model composes heatStepGrid_abs_le perfectly.

pairs.easy_surface: The final convex-combination weights are exposed after two inner Lax-Friedrichs steps.
pairs.hard_surface: The same third step is written only through nested Lax-Friedrichs step APIs.
pairs.equivalence_note: Both sides prove the same three-step CFL Linf bound under the same CFL and input-bound assumptions.
pairs.risk_note: Designed to improve on the saturated v3 wrapper pairs by requiring multiple one-step bound compositions.

pairs.easy_surface: The final upwind convex weights are exposed while the two-step neighbor values remain named.
pairs.hard_surface: The same proof obligation is presented as a nested three-step upwind API expression.
pairs.equivalence_note: Both sides prove the same three-step upwind Linf bound under the same CFL and input bounds.
pairs.risk_note: Good companion to V4P04; checks whether directionality and asymmetric stencil choreography matter.

pairs.easy_surface: The two-update mass preservation theorem is stated with the discreteMass API.
pairs.hard_surface: The same two-update mass preservation fact is stated as exposed range-sum zero change.
pairs.equivalence_note: Both sides compose the same KdV boundary-flux mass-preservation lemma twice.
pairs.risk_note: High-value conservation pair because it requires carrying two boundary-flux hypotheses through composition.

pairs.easy_surface: Two residual orthogonality terms are exposed separately.
pairs.hard_surface: The same orthogonality fact is stated for the residual of u+v with the weighted average exposed.
pairs.equivalence_note: Both sides reduce to weighted P0 residual orthogonality under the same nonzero mass assumption.
pairs.risk_note: Targets finite-sum and denominator choreography; reviewer note must explain the additive residual equivalence.

pairs.easy_surface: The outer weighted finite sum is exposed and the inner finiteRowApply remains as a scalar.
pairs.hard_surface: The same two-stage bound is hidden behind nested finiteRowApply APIs.
pairs.equivalence_note: Both sides apply the same finite weighted absolute-sum bound to the inner row and then the outer row.
pairs.risk_note: Useful general finite-index pair; no-LLM baseline must confirm it is not solved by simp alone.

pairs.easy_surface: The three-stage two-row computation is written with row2Apply at every layer.
pairs.hard_surface: The same computation is written with rowStochastic2First wrappers at every layer.
pairs.equivalence_note: Both sides compose the same row2Apply absolute-value stability lemma across three stages.
pairs.risk_note: Extends the earlier two-stage pair to reduce saturation risk.

pairs.easy_surface: Three direct leapfrog invariant-preservation steps are composed explicitly.
pairs.hard_surface: The same three invariant-preservation steps must be recovered from an orbit recurrence and shifted Nat indices.
pairs.equivalence_note: Both sides prove three-step invariant preservation by repeated use of the same one-step invariant lemma.
pairs.risk_note: High-value index-transport pair; Lean Nat normalization must remain a proof-engineering issue, not a mathematical strengthening.
